# Parvaz

> Android VPN app that tunnels phone traffic through a user-deployed
> Cloudflare Worker via a raw-TCP WebSocket with domain fronting.
> Farsi-first UX designed for Iranian users with no technical background.
> Monorepo: Kotlin front end + Go sidecar + JavaScript Worker.

## Overview

Parvaz ships as one APK. Inside it: a Kotlin/Compose UI (Farsi default),
an Android `VpnService` subclass, `tun2socks`, and a Go sidecar
(`libparvaz.so`) that runs a local SOCKS5 server on `127.0.0.1:1080`
and smuggles traffic through a Cloudflare Worker. One-tap onboarding:
scan a `parvaz://` QR code (received via Telegram from a technical
helper) and tap Connect.

The backend is a **Cloudflare Worker** (~50 lines of JavaScript) using
the `cloudflare:sockets` API for raw outbound TCP — bytes pass through
opaque, no MITM, full HTTPS coverage. The app originally planned to use
Google Apps Script, but that path is blocked on Android because
`UrlFetchApp.fetch()` cannot tunnel TLS and user-CA trust is per-app.

## Technology Stack

- Kotlin 2.2.x + Jetpack Compose (Material3), min SDK 24, target SDK 36
- Go 1.24+ (stdlib first; deps: `github.com/coder/websocket`)
- Android VpnService API + tun2socks
- Gradle 9.3.x (Kotlin DSL) + AGP 9.1.x
- Cloudflare Workers with `cloudflare:sockets` API (free tier)
- Light-theme NOTAM / declassified-briefing aesthetic
- Fonts: Vazirmatn (Persian default), Redaction (Latin display), JetBrains Mono

## Architecture

- `app/` — Kotlin + Compose UI (Farsi-first), VpnService subclass, tun2socks wrapper, sidecar launcher, settings (SharedPreferences + EncryptedSharedPreferences), NOTAM theme
- `core/fronter/` — TLS-with-custom-SNI dialer + HTTP/1.1 client (used for the WebSocket upgrade)
- `core/relay/` — WebSocket tunnel to the Cloudflare Worker; implements `socks5.Dialer`
- `core/socks5/` — local SOCKS5 listener
- `core/cmd/parvazd/` — sidecar main, packaged per-ABI as `libparvaz.so`
- `core/protocol/` + `core/codec/` — reserved for future control channel / HTTP interception (not on critical path)
- `worker/worker.js` — Cloudflare Worker server (TCP piping + access-key validation)
- `reference/` — MasterHttpRelayVPN upstream, read-only historical
- `website/` — bilingual GitHub Pages (EN + FA)

## Key Files

- `CLAUDE.md` — architecture, layer rules, required skills, engineering principles
- `PLAN.md` — TDD milestone list (Phase A core, Phase B app, Phase C integration)
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — full data path diagram and core/app boundary
- `core/go.mod` — Go module `github.com/cocodedk/parvaz/core`
- `worker/worker.js` — the 50-line TCP-passthrough Worker
- `version.txt` — semantic version

## Wire Protocol

```
Per SOCKS5 CONNECT:
    TCP connect <cloudflare_ip>:443
    TLS handshake  SNI = <popular-cf-hosted-site>
    HTTP upgrade → wss://<worker>.workers.dev/tunnel?k=<key>&host=<t>&port=<p>
    Worker opens connect({hostname:<t>, port:<p>}), pipes bytes both ways
```

No JSON envelope. No base64. No batching. Transparent TCP.

## Access URL format

`parvaz://<worker-host>/<access-key>#<optional-display-name>`

Registered as a URI scheme by the Android app — Telegram/WhatsApp deep
links open Parvaz directly; same format encoded in QR codes.

## Links

- Repo: https://github.com/cocodedk/parvaz
- Releases: https://github.com/cocodedk/parvaz/releases/latest
- Website: https://cocodedk.github.io/parvaz/
- Historical upstream: https://github.com/masterking32/MasterHttpRelayVPN (product pivoted away)
- Built by: [Cocode](https://cocode.dk) · [Babak Bandpey](https://linkedin.com/in/babakbandpey)

## Legal / ToS

Cloudflare Workers' acceptable-use policy is strict about proxy
services. Deploy to your own Cloudflare account only. Personal,
research, and educational use only.
